body:
terraswallows:

I don’t even know how to explain how deeply I ache for someone’s touch right now. It’s this quiet, constant hunger under my skin, like my body remembers something my life doesn’t have. I feel it in the empty space beside me, in the way my arms have no one to wrap around, in how badly I just want to be held.

I want to lie next to someone, to feel their arms around me, pulling me close like I belong there. I want to melt into their chest, to breathe them in, to exist in that gentle, wordless closeness where nothing else matters.

I crave the small things so badly it almost hurts, someone leaning into me without thinking, our legs tangled together, sharing quiet moments on a couch while some movie plays in the background neither of us are really watching. I want to feel their head resting on me, or mine on them, that slow drifting into sleep where you don’t even notice when it happens.

That’s what I want, to be held like I’m wanted. Like I’m chosen. Like I’m not alone.

Instead, it’s just this distance. This empty, aching space where they should be. And all I can do is imagine what it would feel like to finally be in someone’s arms, to finally stop feeling this touch-starved and just… be loved.

I want to lie next to someone, to feel their arms around me, pulling me close like I belong there. I want to melt into their chest, to breathe them in, to exist in that gentle, wordless closeness where nothing else matters.

I crave the small things so badly it almost hurts, someone leaning into me without thinking, our legs tangled together, sharing quiet moments on a couch while some movie plays in the background neither of us are really watching.
